rulings.Case Title: L.E.K. CONSULTING, LLC VS. RUBINSTEIN LAW GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rubinstein Law Group'S Motion To Compel Satisfaction Of Judgment And For Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 5, 2012, Line 20. Defendant Rubinstein Law Group'S Motion To Compel Satisfaction Of Judgment And For Attorneys. Granted. Plaintiff's request for fees and costs is untimely because it was filed after paymnent of the judgment amount. Lucky United Properties Investment Corp. v. Lee (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 125 did not hold that costs are automatically incorporated into the judgment. Rather, the court held that unchallenged cost memoranda that were timely filed and an award of attorney's fees granted upon filing a noticed motion are incorporated into the judgment. Here, no cost memorandum or motion for attorney's fees was filed prior to satisfaction of the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL HARRIS VS. ALBERT A LEVY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Continued Motion And Motion For Order To Discharge Deft Stakeholder
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 5, 2012, line 1. DEFENDANT ALBERT LEVY Motion For Order To Discharge Defendant Stakeholder. Granted. The relief requested by the motion gives effect to the order enforcing the settlement filed June 2, 2011, while providing a means and inducement for the plaintiff and the VA to adjudicate the validity of the VA lien. Plaintiff and the VA should meet and confer to determine if they can agree that any or all of the settlement monies to be paid by defendant in excess of the VA's lien can be paid directly to plaintiff rather than deposited in court. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHEL RANTISSI VS. FRANK SHATTUCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses, Without Objections To Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2012, line 2, PLAINTIFF MICHEL RANTISSI Ntc Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses, Without Objections To Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories - Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling responses by Washington Mutual Bank without objections to Plaintiff's Requests for Admission - Set One, and Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - Set One is GRANTED. Defendant Washington Mutual Bank is ordered to serve complete responses, without objections, to Plaintiff's Requests for Admission - Set One, and Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - Set One within 30 days of entry of this Order. Plaintiff's counsel's declaration states that he never received responses to the propounded discovery. The proofs of service on all of the discovery responses submitted for the Court's consideration as Exhibits 2 and 3 to the Declaration of David L. Chavez in Support of Opposition to Motion for an Order Compelling Responses show that the responses were not mailed to Plaintiff's counsel's address. They were mailed to "Abdallah Law Group, 1006 4th Street, 4th Floor, Sacramento, CA 94814." Plaintiff's counsel's address on both discovery requests was listed as "Abdallah Law Group, P.C., 555 Capitol Mall, Suite 725, Sacramento, CA 95814." That is also the address of record for Abdallah Law Group in the Court's Register of Actions for this case. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. The Court is unable to ascertain from the declaration of Plaintiff's counsel how many hours Plaintiff's counsel has spent on the matter and how much time his staff has spent on the matter. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Ernst Halperin, Discovery Judge Pro-tem, Not reported.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES RYAN VS. OOC INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION (D.B.A. EXTREME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 5, 2012, line 3. DEFENDANT OOC INC., TODD PARENT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the second and tenth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the first and third cause of action as to defendant Todd Parent only. Liberally construed, the first cause of action purports to state a claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy and that claim is adequately pled (Collier v. Superior Court (1991) 228 Cal. App. 3d 1117), but, like the third cause of aciton for Labor Code violations, only lies against an employer. The second and tenth claims are not barred by the workers' compensation exclusivity rule because of the allegations, liberally construed, that defendants' conduct contravenes fundamental California policies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SALESFORCE.COM INC. VS. GLOBAL QUEST GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Salesforce.Com Inc.'S (Amended) Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachemnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 5, 2012, Line 23. Plaintiff Salesforce.Com Inc.'S (Amended) Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
